# forktrust

> Safe-by-default git worktree manager for parallel AI coding agents. Refuse-on-conflict merges. Never-lose-WIP guarantee. Pre-flight refusal (no partial-state failures). Per-worktree port allocation. One command (`forktrust agent-docs`) to teach any AI how to use it.

Install: `brew install binkovsky/forktrust/forktrust`

## Documentation tree

The canonical reference lives in `docs/`. Each file is a complete chapter; together they cover every flag, every behavior, every exit code, every JSON field.

- [docs/README.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/README.md): index and glossary
- [docs/getting-started.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/getting-started.md): install → register → first worktree → first finish
- [docs/commands.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/commands.md): every command, every flag, every behavior with examples
- [docs/config.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/config.md): `.forktrustconfig` reference — `[ports]`, `[[hooks.post_create]]`, trust gate
- [docs/exit-codes.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/exit-codes.md): all 14 exit codes with cause, remedy, and agent action
- [docs/json-schema.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/json-schema.md): every JSON output schema with field types and stability rules
- [docs/safety-model.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/safety-model.md): pre-flight refusal, dry-run parity, never-lose-WIP, refuse-on-conflict, refuse-on-ignored-loss, verify gate, scope gate, `.env.local` ownership, trust gate, path safety
- [docs/scope.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/scope.md): change contracts — declare which file globs a task may modify; `finish` refuses out-of-scope edits
- [docs/pr.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/pr.md): PR mode — `forktrust pr` opens a GitHub PR via `gh`; `forktrust pr-status` reports CI / approvals / mergeable
- [docs/workflows.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/workflows.md): canonical patterns — parallel tasks, dirty main, abandon-and-restore, multi-repo, CI
- [docs/troubleshooting.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/troubleshooting.md): every error message and exit code mapped to a fix
- [docs/ai-integration.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/ai-integration.md): Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, Codex recipes; future MCP server design
- [docs/shell-integration.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/shell-integration.md): `ft()` function, tab completion, fzf picker, prompts

## Top-level

- [README](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/README.md): hero, install, comparison table vs gtr/gwq/wtp/workz/claude-squad/conductor, roadmap
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/AGENTS.md): AI-agent hub (concise pointers into docs/)
- [LICENSE](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/LICENSE): MIT

## Workflow

```bash
forktrust new <slug>                       # create isolated worktree
ft <slug>                                  # cd in via the shell function
# ... edit files (NOT in main checkout) ...
forktrust finish <slug>                    # commit + merge + push + cleanup
```

Abandon (snapshots WIP first):
```bash
forktrust rm <slug>                        # → wip/<branch>-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-<sha7>
```

## Seven hard guarantees

1. **Pre-flight refusal** — non-zero exit ⇒ no git mutation happened.
2. **Dry-run parity** — `--dry-run --json`'s `would_refuse` exactly matches real-command behavior (exception: verify is not executed in dry-run; scope IS).
3. **Never-lose-WIP** — `rm` pushes to `wip/<branch>-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-<sha7>` before removing.
4. **Refuse-on-conflict** — `finish` aborts merge on conflict (exit 2). Never `--strategy`.
5. **Refuse-on-ignored-loss** — exit 14 if `git worktree remove` would silently delete ignored files.
6. **Verify gate** — `[verify]` section in `.forktrustconfig` declares commands that must exit zero before `finish` may merge. Exit 15 on failure. Bypass: `finish --no-verify` (warned).
7. **Scope gate (change contract)** — per-worktree scope file (`forktrust new --scope "globs"` or `forktrust scope --set`) limits which files the task may modify. Exit 16 if the diff is out-of-scope. Bypass: `finish --no-scope` (warned).

`.env.local` is treated as forktrust-managed only if (a) regular file at worktree root, (b) starts with the exact `# Managed by forktrust...` header.

## Stable exit codes (will not change across releases)

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| 0 | success |
| 2 | merge conflict (refuse to auto-resolve) |
| 3 | main worktree is dirty |
| 4 | push failed |
| 5 | wip/* push failed (worktree NOT removed) |
| 6 | no worktree matching slug |
| 7 | slug matches multiple projects |
| 8 | hook failed or untrusted command hook |
| 9 | no origin remote configured |
| 10 | main checkout on wrong branch |
| 11 | cwd is in unregistered git repo |
| 12 | rm/finish could not determine ahead count |
| 13 | rm/finish: worktree removed but branch -D failed |
| 14 | rm/finish refused: ignored files in worktree would be permanently deleted |
| 15 | finish refused: [verify] gate failed (command non-zero or require_clean post-verify dirty) |
| 16 | finish/scope-check refused: worktree diff touches files outside declared --scope contract |
| 17 | pr/pr-status: gh CLI not available (install gh, or run gh auth login) |
| 18 | pr: gh pr create returned non-zero |

Full action table: [docs/exit-codes.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/exit-codes.md).

## Shell integration

```bash
# ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc
ft() {
  local p
  p="$(forktrust cd "$1" 2>/dev/null)" || { echo "forktrust: no worktree '$1'" >&2; return 1; }
  cd "$p" || return 1
}
```

Or `forktrust shell <slug>` for a subshell with `FORKTRUST_SLUG` exported. Full guide: [docs/shell-integration.md](https://github.com/binkovsky/forktrust/blob/main/docs/shell-integration.md).
